false_form: The relativization barrier (Baker-Gill-Solovay 1975) implies that purely mathematical approaches cannot resolve P vs NP, requiring physical or logical-foundation changes.
name: RELATIVIZATION_BARRIER_TOTALITY
true_form: The relativization barrier (Baker-Gill-Solovay 1975) rules out only proof strategies that hold relative to any arbitrary black-box oracle. It is formally bypassed by non-black-box techniques: arithmetization (IP = PSPACE, Shamir 1992); interactive proof systems exploiting algebraic structure of polynomial encodings; circuit-specific lower bound techniques operating directly on uniform structure. The barrier is a constraint on a proof class, not on mathematics as a whole.
verification_source: Gemini Deep Research pilot DR-S001 2026-05-14; primary citation Baker-Gill-Solovay 1975 SIAM J. Comput. 4(4):431-442 (DOI 10.1137/0204037); cross-reference Aaronson-Wigderson 2008 'Algebrization' survey.
